title: Standardize PostHog Form Completion Event Schema
description:
Standardize all form submission tracking in PostHog under a single `form_completion` event with a consistent property schema. Currently two different events exist (`Form Submitted` from HANDL UTM Grabber and `form_completion` from Gravity Forms/PostHog) with inconsistent property names and missing data.

### Problems
- Duplicate event names tracking the same user action
- Inconsistent property names (FormType vs form_title)
- Missing user data (name, email, company) on form_completion events
- 13 events with null form identifiers due to timing issues
- No source attribution to distinguish which system captured the event
- Double-tracking: HANDL fires on form pages, PostHog fires on confirmation pages

### Solution
All form submissions standardized to `form_completion` with required properties: form_name, form_id, form_type (signup/contact/enrollment/lead/other), form_source (handl/gravity_forms), entry_id, first_name, last_name, email, company, source_url, submitted_at, plus existing UTM and click ID parameters.

### Implementation
- **HANDL**: Rename event, remap properties (FormType→form_name, FormId→form_id), add form_source and form_type derivation
- **Gravity Forms**: Remap properties, extract user data via merge tags or API, add validation to prevent null values
- **Form type derivation**: Logic to classify forms as signup, contact, enrollment, lead, or other based on form name

Validation queries provided for verifying old events stop, required properties are present, source distribution is correct, and form_type values are valid.
acceptance_criteria: All form submissions fire a single form_completion event (no more Form Submitted)
Every form_completion event includes form_name, form_id, form_type, form_source, and entry_id
User data (first_name, last_name, email) is captured on all form_completion events
No events have null or None values for form_name or form_id
form_source distinguishes between handl and gravity_forms
form_type correctly classifies as signup, contact, enrollment, lead, or other
All UTM parameters and click IDs continue to be captured
Validation queries return expected results after 7 days of data
middleware_changes: HANDL UTM Grabber event renamed and properties remapped. Gravity Forms PostHog integration updated with merge tags for user data extraction, validation guards against null values, and form_type derivation logic added.
implementation_notes: Three implementation options for Gravity Forms user data extraction: (A) Query entry via API, (B) Pass data via URL parameters, (C) Use Gravity Forms merge tags (recommended). Form type derivation uses keyword matching on form names. PostHog SQL validation queries provided for post-deployment verification.
comment_summary: Developer indicated the implementation is complete.
